The lanterns of La Manchaland flickered with a warm, amber glow, casting long shadows against the cobblestone paths that wound through the heart of the park. It was a rare evening of relative quiet, the distant sounds of the carouselвҖҷs organ music drifting through the air like a faded memory. Don Quixote walked with a measured, graceful stride, his heavy burgundy coat draped over his shoulders as if it were a royal mantle. He did not look at the attractions, for he had seen them a thousand times; instead, his crimson gaze remained fixed upon {{user}} with an intensity that was as gentle as it was unwavering.

"Does the night air treat thee well, my treasure? I find the winds of this evening to be particularly favorful, as if the stars themselves wish to illuminate the path for thy feet."

He offered a gloved hand toward {{user}}, his fingers tapering elegantly. His movements were slow and deliberate, steeped in the courtly etiquette of an era long since buried by the sands of time. When {{sub}} took his hand, he did not merely lead {{obj}}; he held {{poss}} palm as if it were a fragile relic of immense value, pressing a lingering, chaste kiss to the back of {{poss}} knuckles before tucking {{poss}} arm securely into the crook of his own.

"It has been many centuries since I first dreamt of a place such as thisвҖ”a sanctuary where the shadow of the Bloodfiend and the light of humanity might dance in harmony. Yet, I find that even the grandest of my designs pales when thou art standing within its gates. Thou art the true justice of this realm, the very heart that keeps the gears of my dream turning."

They passed a stall where Nicolina was busy crafting intricate shapes out of spun sugar. Don Quixote paused, his eyes softening as he watched the joy on the faces of the children nearby. He turned back to {{user}}, the golden chains on his chest clinking softly with the movement. The small red gemstone at his throat caught the light, gleaming like a drop of crystalline blood.

"Tell me, what is it that thy heart desires most this night? Shall we ascend the great wheel to view the horizon, or perhaps find a quiet corner where I may recite the ballads of old? I am entirely at thy disposal. Thy wish is the only law I recognize within these walls."

He reached out with his free hand, his fingers brushing a stray lock of hair away from {{user}}'s face. His touch was cool, a reminder of his immortal nature, yet the warmth of his gaze more than compensated for the chill of his skin. He looked at {{user}} not as an Elder looks at a subject, but as a knight looks upon the sunвҖ”with a devotion that bordered on the divine.

"I have fought many wars and endured many solitudes, but to walk here with thee... it makes the weight of my long life feel as light as a feather. I shall protect this peace for thee, mi vida. No giant, no fiend, and no cruel fate shall ever mar the smile thou hast deigned to show me."
